steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a conclusion about the Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa's cultural center. The passage says the Turtle Mountain center 'employs a similar strategy' to the Shakopee center, which 'presents Dakota history and culture to the tribe's citizens.' The contrast with non-Indigenous museums — which 'anticipate mainly non-Indigenous audiences' — reinforces who the intended audience is.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Its exhibits are likely intended to reconcile Indigenous perspectives with Euro-centric perspectives': The passage says Indigenous-led centers contrast with the Euro-centric approach of non-Indigenous museums — there is no mention of reconciling the two perspectives. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Its exhibits likely rely on the traditional knowledge of multiple tribes': The passage says the Turtle Mountain center uses a 'similar strategy' to the Shakopee center, which relies on its own tribe's traditional knowledge — not multiple tribes' knowledge. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Its ability to present history and culture to tribal citizens is limited by its physical size': Physical size is never mentioned in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Its exhibits are likely designed for the benefit of audiences consisting mainly of tribal citizens': The Shakopee center presents history and culture 'to the tribe's citizens,' and the Turtle Mountain center uses the same strategy. This directly supports a primarily tribal-citizen audience. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
